*Best indian film out recommendation except RRR ( Tollywood )*

👉 Bajrangi Bhaijan ( Bollywood ) - from the same scriptwriter of RRR . RRR director was supposed to direct this but couldn't due to busy schedule .

👉URI:- The surgical strike (Bollywood)
Based on true story of Indian army surgical strike mission

👉 Lagaan ( Bollywood ) - received Oscars nomination . About British colonial rule .

👉3 Idiots (Bollywood) :- Story of a 3 Indian Engineering students

👉 777 Charlie ( Sandalwood ) - dog and human friendship .

👉 Krish 3 (Bollywood)
Best Indian superhero Flim..

👉 Sardar Udham ( Bollywood ) - based on real incident during British colonial rule . 

👉Dangal ( Bollywood ) - highest grossing non - Hollywood foreign film in China . Highest grossing Indian film worldwide .

Upcoming Most Anticipated :

author: @harshtur
text: The whole bridge sequence is cool but at the same time, it also summarizes the whole movie plot. Ram's mission is regarding the country (jumps with a Flag) and Bheem's mission is to save the child from his tribe (jumps to get the child) and in this journey they exchange their missions (exchange of flag and child) and eventually succeed helping each other.

replies.author: @CultureCasino
replies.text: Brilliant analysis.... Spot On...

replies.author: @harshtur
replies.text: @CultureCasino Another observation- throughout the first half Bheem chooses to stay in muslim household disguised in the name of Akhtar and carries Beef inside the house on regular basis. Raju thinks it is for Akhtar and his households consumption but he feeds the beef to Wild tigers and leopards instead to plan the attack on British to free Malli. We as viewers dont realise this until the interval scene where we see him freeing Malli with help of wild Animals.

replies.author: @AnonymousBVed
replies.text: How funny. We South Indians tend to be tired of caricature-like villains and heroes. Almost all our movies have this over-the-top action and black and white moral view of the world. Not denying that RRR is very entertaining tho.

replies.author: @PankajGoelArtist
replies.text: @AnonymousBVed What you describe is what even I had also felt. The thing is in last 3 decades of living and travelling around the western countries I found out that these were the reasons everyone I talked to would be willing to watch any film except an Indian film. RRR changed that in style. There is a big reason for that. The key lies in analysis of the hooks it has early on where it hooked people in so much that later on when there were things which had technical issues but by that time it did not matter. Hollywood needs to learn from this. All scriptwriters worldwide need to learn from this.

replies.author: @AnonymousBVed
replies.text: @PankajGoelArtist True. RRR does well in establishing stakes and character motivations. In the typical Telugu movie of superhuman hero-in love with gangster's daughter-fighting some criminal or corrupt politician, there's no interest because the hero is never vulnerable. We know he's gonna single-handedly beat up all the rowdies and get the girl. But RRR shows us early on that our heroes can be hurt. Their motivation for fighting also makes sense and it's consistent.

replies.author: @johngibson8636
replies.text: @AnonymousBVed In the US, movies have become garbage over the last 5 years, men are portrayed as incapable and need to be saved by a smarter and stronger woman.  Which is fine on its own, but here it is in everything.  There are no more male Heroes.  That being said Top Gun Maverick is the first movie in a long time where it shows competent and capable people regardless of their gender.  The other thing that they have done is to take villains classic stories, and then build a story about how they are just misunderstood and you should be sympathetic to them.  So movies like RRR for people in the US is a breath of fresh air.  It's the kind of movie we used to get.

replies.text: I am from india and i loved doctor strange........but set of was they didn't explain doctor strange powers they made it like he can do everything....... heroes need to have constraints in their power......... that's what i love about avatar the last Airbender that they choreograph very well that entire arcs feels a journey.......and with well established power scale.......and that's what American movies does so well........is that establishing power scale and limitations and than hero somehow figures out a way to bypass that........ that's what makes a good super hero movie........in indian movies it's journey of different kind.......focus is more on feeling rather than plotting.........

author: @scyber_avatar
text:
Note; Here are basics about Indian Cinema that are ignored in WEST bcz of exaggerated stereotypes.
Indian Cinema is ON since 1910s ... Unlike USA which has ONLY ONE H0llywood ... India has 20+ different film industries based on different languages/regions .. B0llywood (Hindi films) is just one of 20+ ... India produces avg 1600+ films per year .. Most in world ... and of almost every style/genre .. not all musicals

author: @lucky-zt8nm
text:
Director took real characters and made a fictional story on them

Facts: Both are from same time period but different places, both went out of their native place for 2yrs and came back to their place and faught against British at their respective places (Both are less than 30 age when they died)

Intersting fact: They never met each other in real life, movie is all about those 2 yrs ( no one don't where they went, so director made a fictional story on it)

Bheem went out of his house as uneducated person and came back as an educated person
Sita Rama Raju Was well educated before

author: @nithiyashree3701
text:
Not all Indian films are epic, over the top, historic and high budget busters.... well, that's what's gaining the high amount of recognition world wide but I'd like you to check some grounded stuff from the southern and the North Indian cinema as well. They're not necessarily bollywood but here are some suggestions from a few directors and Actors where you can start from 
1.  Films of Anurag Kashyap (Gangs of Wasseypur part 1,2)
2. Shoojith Sircar's "Sardar Udham" (Got rejected for an oscar submission due to political reasons of how the film potrays against someone but it's very dumb of them to cancel on those aspects since we have films like Schindlers' list)
3. Lokesh Kanagaraj's Kaithi and Vikram.... (A universe of it's own)
4. Hey Ram by Kamal Haasan ( Like literally, he wrote, acted, directed, produced and is also a dance, make up artist, choreographer, Technician, singer and a lot of other things you can explore) 
5. And for nice romantic films that hollywood might not have ever done or seen is a film called 96 by Vijay sethupathi and Trisha

author: @satyanarayanarajuraju8089
text:
Some details about the movie characters : 

Originally Alluri Sita Rama Raju, wasn't a tribal himself, he went into forests for helping tribals, actually used to wear Saffron clothing and used bow & arrows resembling Hindu god "RAM" (was also a  sharp shooter) It was his approach to stand as a symbol of inspiration for tribals to revolt (known as Rampa Rebellion, with motto "Mande Rajyam" which translates to.. Ours is this kingdom) against British in Visakhapatnam agency.  (Some believe that Alluri Rama Raju taking "Sanyas" (divine way of living) after death of his love Sita, was the reason for his Saffron attire). Sita really was his lover's name and after her death he changed his name from Alluri Rama Raju to Alluri Sita Rama Raju.
Born in the West-Godavari (a district of present Indian state Andhra Pradesh), in a Telugu Kshatriya family to Alluri Venkata Rama Raju and his wife Suryanarayanamma, after his parents died (when he was 12) was raised by his uncle Rama krishnam Raju. He was a well educated, civilized man who felt that the tribals are the ones being suppressed the most by british, and went to forests and dedicated his entire life for helping them fight british, although wasn't a tribal himself.
He used to do guerilla raids on British police stations (after informing & challenging them that he's going to come & attack and stop him if they can) and collect guns & weapons, using an army of armed tribals by training them.
It's said that it costed more than a million even in those days, for the british to finally find and execute him. They killed Rama Raju unethically while he's unarmed, performing a morning Prayer.

Komaram Bheem, a tribal himself from gond tribes of Adilabad (a district of the present Indian state "Telangana") is rumoured to be an extremely strong man, and to hunt a tiger by himself. He used to wear muslim disguises during his revolt against Nizam. He died fighting the Nizam's of Bhagyanagaram (Present day Hyderabad)

 In Telugu states (Andhra, Telangana) everyone knows these things, maybe not in other states. They were referred as Sri Ram and Bheemasen (since long back before the movie even existed !!) from their names and features (Rama Raju's- Saffron Attire, his lover's name is "Sita", went to forests and made an army using tribals, man of extreme commitment, Komaram Bheem- Physical strength, Crude attacking nature, Perseverance) being similar to characteristics of Lord Ram from Ramayanam and Bheem from Mahabharatam and out of the level of reverence they've achieved from people. 

Both left their homes almost in the same period and then returned after some time and started their revolutions against British and Nizam respectively. After returning home, Rama Raju started wearing Saffron clothing till the day he died fighting for tribals, and Bheem came back as an educated man and started his rebellion with motto.. Jal, Jangal, Jameen.. with translates to Water, Forest & Soil..
As no one has a clear idea about what happened to them in that period when they left, and what made them become revolutionaries.. the movie writer wrote a Fictional story that they met and became friends and Bheem was the one who gave Rama Raju a Saffron attire and that he got educated himself from Rama Raju, connecting everything that was remembered in telugu people hearts.. if we see carefully, we can see all these details used in the movie clearly.. The mythological references are used in bgms..  as these freedom fighters are actually revered by referring them as Lord Sri Rama and Bheemasen.. in many parts of Telugu states...

Other details :
-Also The Indian Flag used throughout the movie was the older Berlin Committee Flag  (designed by Madame Bhikaji Cama, Vinayak Damodar Savarkar and Shyamji Krishna Varma) corresponding to the time period.
(Current Indian flag was designed by Pingali Venkayya, also from Telugu land)
-Rama Raju is also referred by people as "Manyam Dhora".. which translates to King/Chief of the Wild Forests and Komaram Bheem is praised as "Gondu Bebbuli".. which translates to Ferocious Tiger of Gond tribes. These names are also referred in the Original (Telugu) movie version  on zee5. The one in Netflix is Hindi dubbed (bollywood) version.
-The bike used by Bheem was the older world war version which were designed lightweight for men to lift and carry them when required.

-Names of Freedom fighters shown in the rrr movie ending song & which indian state they belong to & their language :
-Subhash Chandra Bose (state west bengal/ orissa, language Bengali)
-Sardar Vallabhai Patel (state Gujarat, language Gujarati)
-Kittur Rani Chennamma (state Karnataka, language kannada)
-V.O.Chidambharam pillai (state Tamil nadu, language Tamil)
-Bhagat Singh (state Punjab, language Punjabi)
-Tanguturi Prakasam panthulu (also RRR Heroes Alluri SitaRama Raju & Komaram Bheem) (state Andhra, language Telugu)
-Varma Pazhassi Raja (state Kerala, language malayalam)
-Chatrapathi Sivaji (state Maharashtra, language Marathi)

author: @sahityabk
text: Interesting listen. I couldnt follow how you got toxic masculinity from the leads tho. It was a small comment on your part but it is a label that is being negatively thrown around these days, so I just want to address it. I didn't see many toxic traits in either of the leads. I saw them as very sincere in their behaviour, not putting up a front for anything. They are not afraid of showing emotion, especially to the people they love. They are very sweet to eachother and openly acknowledge their strong friendship and love for each other at the risk of being vulnerable, especially Bheem. Ram is only more stoic cuz of his scarred history and his ultimate goal, and its not to put up a front to seem always be in control or incharge. It's all for a sincere and justified purpose. He even gave up winning in the danceoff for his friend. Defiinately not toxic. Neither of them seem to disrespect women either. Lol when i was watching this, I felt so happy in how not toxic their characters were.

author: @Rgvf793
text:
Most of us Indians missed RRR, because of Kashmir Files. Just like the fan following for RRR in US, people promoted Kashmir Files in India through word of mouth. And the hype near Kashmir Files overpowered RRR which was released immediately after K.F. Thus it didn't create the impact on Indian Audience as much as Bahubali 2.
BTW you people may be won't like K.F because it's only relatable to Indians.

It made me believe RRR is not for me and what a loss.

I wish I could have seen it in the theatres. I saw it digitally. And loved it so much!!

They promoted it SO BAD.

replies.author: @harshau8332
replies.text:
@MrGrantGregory Tollywood (Telugu language, from south of India) movies are over the top but entertaining. Just like RRR, our movies doesn't fit into one genre. Most of the movies are 'hero' centric. Big hero movies mostly deals with societal problems but they are not preachy. If you can bare over the top fights like in RRR and mixed genre (masala movies) then check out
Bahubali 1 and 2
Eega
Srimanthudu
Major
Gudachari
Maharshi
Vedam
Rangasthalam
Janata garage
Bhagamati

Entertainment guaranteed. These are from only Telugu film industry. There are great movies from Tamil, Malayalam and kannada is catching up too.

Tamil and Telugu are alike. Over the top, but great narration and mixed genres. 

Malayalam, script based, low budget, critically acclaimed. 

Kannada - kgf 1 and 2. Don't know any other. Sorry

Bollywood- Hindi: world renowned, but not so great movies now a days. Going thtough a phase like Hollywood. 
Dangal
Tare zameen par
3 idiots
PK
Jodha akbar

author: @joydeep3928
text:
*Best indian film out recommendation except RRR ( Tollywood )* 
:👉 Bahubali 1 & 2 ( Tollywood ) - from the same director and scriptwriter of RRR .

👉 Bajrangi Bhaijan ( Bollywood ) - from the same scriptwriter of RRR . RRR director was supposed to direct this but couldn't due to busy schedule .

👉URI:- The surgical strike (Bollywood)
Based on true story of Indian army surgical strike mission

👉 Lagaan ( Bollywood ) - received Oscars nomination . About British colonial rule .

👉3 Idiots (Bollywood) :- Story of a 3 Indian Engineering students

👉 777 Charlie ( Sandalwood ) - dog and human friendship .

👉 Krish 3 (Bollywood)
Best Indian superhero Flim..

👉 Sardar Udham ( Bollywood ) - based on real incident during British colonial rule . 

👉Dangal ( Bollywood ) - highest grossing non - Hollywood foreign film in China . Highest grossing Indian film worldwide .

Upcoming Most Anticipated : 
👉Brahmastra ( Bollywood ) - Trailer out (sept 9)
 👉Ponniyin Selvan ( Kollywood ) teaser out (30 sept)
👉Aadhipurush 
Releasing on January 2023🙂❤️😍

text: I have done quite a bit of analysis on RRR to find out why it became the first Indian film to hook American and English audience in. This film has laid out so many traps early on to hook English speaking audience.

replies.author: @PankajGoelArtist
replies.text: @One-pieceisbestanimeintheworld Thank you. I have so much more analysis for RRR, as its a rare world event that has changed the way west sees cinema from now on.

replies.author: @PankajGoelArtist
replies.text: @CultureCasino True. But there are tons of films that fit that criteria. RRR plays on western minds early on like no one else does. As an example, it does OTT action, song and dance numbers which put off western audiences and yet it manages to hook them in early on in a way that later on when those things come around by the time people are so hooked it does not matter.

author: @OneManShow1964
text: From the scenes shown, this film does not hit a spot in my 'favored genre' list. Still, I will give a taste test and see what I think of the flavor. For what it is worth, Indiana Jones did not thrill me all that much.

replies.author: @ObtuseRubber
replies.text: WE AGREE !

replies.author: @OneManShow1964
replies.text: Had a three hour blackout (a particularly hot day, today, so not a surprise). I did watch it, thanks to a UPS box, a couple of car batteries and a power inverter. I did like the music. High marks, there. The bros' miraculous ability to recover from injury kept breaking what immersion I was able to achieve. Ray Stevenson was a bit of a surprise, even though his role was relatively small. The plot, however, was no surprise at all, as was the ridiculous dance-off. Frankly, I enjoyed Veer more than RRR. To me, RRR was fair. Not good, and certainly not great. Culture had me intrigued. I should have known better. With that said, I have recently watched far worse things.

author: @the_winter_soldier477
text: Guys, Please watch "Gangs of Wasseypur 1 and 2". It's one of the greatest movie to have come out of the Indian Cinema. The movies is set in the hinterlands of India spanning decades and generations, and the story is about the coal mafias and politicians. Trust me after watching this movie...you will have a whole new appreciation for the Indian cinema.I know rrr is alll about bravado, bromance and epicness but this is movie is on a different level and the acting in this movie is some of the best you will see in the whole wide world. You will not be disappointed that's for sure. Even, Martin Scorcese applauded this movie.

author: @avijitgoswami3082
text:
Mythology ramayana ..
Bhim symbol lord Hanuman who carry lord Ram to kill devil ravana ..
Ram was best Archer in hindu mythology ... 
Also Hanuman destroyed ravanas kingdom , ravana palace ..
Here also Bheem came with animals to destroy british palace ..
Though ram and Hanuman never fights with each other .. also Ram's wife Sita was kidnapped by ravana .. here .. little girl ...
Also Hanuman takes parts of Himalaya and put in Sri Lanka for ayurvedic herb to ram and laxman .... 
They mix british colonial history with mythology ramayana with additional things also some opposite things ..... 
That's why they have superpower as a human ..
They represent mythological characters and strength ...
Lord Ram was cool calm guy with best Archer talent and incarnation of lord Vishnu .. 
Hanuman was part of lord Shiva .. Hanuman have huge body power ... Like 10000 Hulk ... 
Still in traditional Indian body building institute puts a murti statue or picture of lord Hanuman ..
Though lord Hanuman is symbol of bachelor and strength ...
Lord Ram was symbol of ideal man ... Adi purush ..
Bheem also symbol a character from  epic Mahabharata, he also was strongest human ..
Both Bheem and lord Hanuman is pawan putra or son of wind god ..
Hanuman is close to monkey god .. it's not human name .. so they used name Bheem as a alter of lord Hanuman ..
They both are son of same wind god ... They are pawan putra

text:
Here are few recommendations of recent 2010s decade from different industries for you to watch :-
HINDI/ B0llywood = Gangs of Wasseypur series, The Lunchbox, Tumbbad, Andhadhun, Uri-The Surgical Strike, Padmaavat, Kahaani
TELUGU/ T0llywood = Bahubali series, Eega, .Agent Sai Sreenivas Athreya, Jersey, Kshanam.
TAMIL/ K0llywood = Super Deluxe, Vikram Vedha, Raatchasan, Kakka Muttai, Visaaranai, Vada Chennai, 96, 2.0.
MALAYALAM/ M0llywood = Kumbalangi Nights, Drishyam series, Jallikattu, Ee. Ma. Yau.
KANNADA/ Sandalwood = KGF series, Lucia, Rangitaranga, Avane srimannarayana, Uturn
OTHERS --- Sairat (MARATHI), Aamis (ASSAMESE), Hellaro (GUJARATI) ..
